United States|Australia.headline: USMNT cruise past Australia in Seattle
United States|Australia.summary: An early Cameron Burgess own goal settled the hosts before Alex Freeman doubled the lead before half-time. The United States controlled the contest and saw out a comfortable 2-0 win to open their account.
United States|Australia.star_reason: Capped a lively display with the decisive second goal on the stroke of half-time.
United States|Australia.turning_point: Burgess's 11th-minute own goal handed the USMNT an early grip on the game.
United States|Australia.what_lacked: Australia created little of note and never tested the US back line.

Scotland|Morocco.headline: Saibari's record-quick strike sinks Scotland
Scotland|Morocco.summary: Ismael Saibari scored after just 72 seconds — the fastest goal of the tournament — and Morocco defended resolutely to hold on for a 1-0 win. Scotland pushed but were denied, with a McTominay penalty appeal waved away.
Scotland|Morocco.star_reason: His 2nd-minute strike was the only goal and set the tone for Morocco's disciplined display.
Scotland|Morocco.turning_point: Brahim Diaz's lofted ball released Saibari inside two minutes.
Scotland|Morocco.what_lacked: Scotland lacked a cutting edge in the final third and missed their chance to qualify.

Brazil|Haiti.headline: Cunha double powers Brazil past Haiti
Brazil|Haiti.summary: Matheus Cunha struck twice in the first half and Vinicius Junior added a third on the stroke of half-time as Brazil eliminated Haiti with a convincing 3-0 win in Philadelphia. Neymar was rested.
Brazil|Haiti.star_reason: Two first-half goals (23', 36') broke the game open and reawakened Selecao hopes.
Brazil|Haiti.turning_point: Cunha's quickfire brace put Brazil in command before the interval.
Brazil|Haiti.what_lacked: Haiti were organised but offered almost no attacking threat against a rotated Brazil.

Paraguay|Turkey.headline: Galarza's early bolt stuns Turkey
Paraguay|Turkey.summary: Matias Galarza scored inside 65 seconds and Paraguay held on — even after Miguel Almiron's first-half red card left them a man down — to beat Turkey 1-0 and eliminate the Turks. Galarza's effort squeezed through Merih Demiral and beat Ugurcan Cakir.
Paraguay|Turkey.star_reason: His sub-2-minute winner was the difference and set a tournament goal record.
Paraguay|Turkey.turning_point: Almiron's straight red after a VAR review forced Paraguay to defend for over an hour.
Paraguay|Turkey.what_lacked: Turkey dominated the ball but could not break down ten-man Paraguay.

Netherlands|Sweden.headline: Dutch firepower overwhelms Sweden
Netherlands|Sweden.summary: Brian Brobbey and Cody Gakpo each scored twice and Crysencio Summerville added a late fifth as the Netherlands raced to a 5-1 win and top of Group F. Anthony Elanga grabbed a consolation for Sweden.
Netherlands|Sweden.star_reason: Two goals in seven second-half minutes (47', 54') put the result beyond doubt.
Netherlands|Sweden.turning_point: Brobbey's 5th-minute opener set an early tone Sweden could never recover from.
Netherlands|Sweden.what_lacked: Sweden had no answer to the Dutch transition and were overrun in midfield.

Germany|Ivory Coast.headline: Undav off the bench rescues Germany
Germany|Ivory Coast.summary: Franck Kessie put Ivory Coast ahead at the break, but substitute Deniz Undav levelled in the 68th minute and snatched a 94th-minute winner to send Germany into the Round of 32 with a dramatic 2-1 comeback.
Germany|Ivory Coast.star_reason: A match-changing brace off the bench, including the stoppage-time winner.
Germany|Ivory Coast.turning_point: Nagelsmann's substitutions (Undav, Amiri, Nmecha) flipped a flat first-half display.
Germany|Ivory Coast.what_lacked: Ivory Coast led at half-time but couldn't manage the game and conceded twice late.

Ecuador|Curaçao.headline: Room's heroics earn Curacao a historic point
Ecuador|Curaçao.summary: Goalkeeper Eloy Room made 15 saves as Curacao held Ecuador to a 0-0 draw for their first-ever World Cup point. Ecuador's 15 shots on target without scoring was the most in a World Cup match since 1966.
Ecuador|Curaçao.star_reason: Fifteen saves — a one-man wall that denied a dominant Ecuador all night.
Ecuador|Curaçao.turning_point: Enner Valencia's two gilt-edged misses summed up Ecuador's frustration.
Ecuador|Curaçao.what_lacked: Ecuador dominated possession and chances but lacked a finishing touch.

Tunisia|Japan.headline: Ueda double sends Japan top, eliminates Tunisia
Tunisia|Japan.summary: Daichi Kamada scored Japan's fastest-ever World Cup goal in the 4th minute and Ayase Ueda struck twice, with Junya Ito also on target, as Japan thrashed Tunisia 4-0 — the first AFC side to score four in a World Cup game. Tunisia were eliminated.
Tunisia|Japan.star_reason: A first-half strike and a late header capped a clinical Japan performance.
Tunisia|Japan.turning_point: Kamada's 4th-minute opener set Japan on their way inside five minutes.
Tunisia|Japan.what_lacked: Tunisia were second best across the pitch and never threatened a route back.

Spain|Saudi Arabia.headline: Yamal and Oyarzabal run riot as Spain cruise past Saudi Arabia
Spain|Saudi Arabia.summary: Lamine Yamal opened inside 10 minutes and Mikel Oyarzabal struck twice in a three-minute spell to put Spain out of sight before half-time. A Hassan Al-Tambakti own goal early in the second half completed a comfortable 4-0 win that sends Spain top of Group H.
Spain|Saudi Arabia.star_reason: A clinical 21st- and 24th-minute brace turned an early lead into a rout.
Spain|Saudi Arabia.turning_point: Oyarzabal's quickfire double midway through the first half killed the contest.
Spain|Saudi Arabia.what_lacked: Saudi Arabia offered little going forward and were undone by Spain’s movement between the lines.

Belgium|Iran.headline: Ten-man Belgium held by stubborn Iran in goalless stalemate
Belgium|Iran.summary: Belgium were frustrated by a disciplined Iran and saw their afternoon unravel when they were reduced to ten men early in the second half. Iran defended deep and dug in for a 0-0 draw that leaves Group G wide open ahead of New Zealand vs Egypt.
Belgium|Iran.star_reason: A compact, organised back line shut out one of the tournament favourites.
Belgium|Iran.turning_point: Belgium's second-half red card forced them onto the back foot.
Belgium|Iran.what_lacked: Belgium lacked a cutting edge in the final third and paid for the dismissal.

Uruguay|Cape Verde.headline: Cape Verde stun Uruguay with late leveller in 2-2 thriller
Uruguay|Cape Verde.summary: Kevin Pina’s 21st-minute free-kick put Cape Verde ahead before Uruguay roared back through Maximiliano Araújo and an Agustín Canobbio strike in first-half stoppage time. Helio Varela’s 61st-minute equaliser earned the Blue Sharks a shock 2-2 draw with La Celeste.
Uruguay|Cape Verde.star_reason: His second-half equaliser snatched a historic point for Cape Verde.
Uruguay|Cape Verde.turning_point: Varela's 61st-minute strike cancelled out Uruguay's late first-half turnaround.
Uruguay|Cape Verde.what_lacked: Uruguay switched off defensively after the break and could not see out a winning position.
